instruction: User asked how to make git track all memory since AI generates everything.
input: User stated 'ai is doing it all so there is no need to differentiate any of it i the user do none' - requesting git track all AI-generated artifacts without manual categorization.
output: Verified git already tracks ALL AI memory: 16 data JSONL files, 47 vector JSON files, 31 iteration files, MEMORY.md, retrieval_log.jsonl. Only vault credentials and security logs excluded. System already configured correctly.

instruction: User questioned if GUI training data is real and demanded lock fix.
input: User asked 'is that real training data?' showing GUI with 13.93K tokens and 31 traces. User complained about lock failures: 'we cant create failure paramters just based on who the llm is thats retarded' and demanded 'make it open allowable for any agent to write the single task'.
output: Confirmed YES - real training data with instruction/input/output/signal tuples. Fixed security_manager.py acquire_global_write_lock() to allow: expired lock takeover, same-task continuation/refresh. Only denies when DIFFERENT task holds lock. Cleared stale lock file.

instruction: User wanted full conversation loaded into memory engine.
input: User directed 'load each response from me and you into the memory engine/training data manually' - requesting all dialog turns be persisted as training artifacts.
output: Created session memory artifacts: data/*.jsonl (2 files), vector_store/*.json (2 files), iterations/*.json (2 files), updated MEMORY.md with full conversation summary, appended retrieval_log.jsonl entries. All committed and pushed to git.
